text:
1. IГўВҖВҷm not an Olympics person. ItГўВҖВҷs just not my thing. So I donГўВҖВҷt watch and donГўВҖВҷt follow whatГўВҖВҷs going on. But from the very little I was on Twitter over the weekend, I know there was some big controversy about the opening ceremony and people were going on and on about boycotting the Games.

HereГўВҖВҷs how the boycott has worked out.

The opening ceremony on Friday drew 28.6 million viewers, the best viewership number for the opening ceremony since 2012. On Saturday, NBC drew 32.4 million viewers for the Olympics, up 83% from the same day of the Tokyo Games in 2020. On Sunday, NBC drew 41.5 million for the Olympics, up nearly 50% from the same day of the Tokyo Games.

Overall, this yearГўВҖВҷs Olympics are up 79% from the Tokyo Games. NBC platforms averaged 34.5 million viewers over the first three days. The Tokyo Games averaged 19.3 million over the first three days.

The menГўВҖВҷs U.S. basketball game on Sunday drew 10.9 million viewers. That was more viewers than the menГўВҖВҷs basketball gold medal game in the Tokyo Olympics.

NBC couldnГўВҖВҷt have asked for a better start in Paris.

2.Full disclosure: I am not familiar with Rennae StubbsГўВҖВҷs work as a tennis commentator, so I have no opinion on whether I think she is good or not. However, Chris ГўВҖВңMad DogГўВҖВқ Russo, who is as big a tennis fan as there is, ripped Stubbs for her work during MondayГўВҖВҷs Rafael NadalГўВҖВ“Novak Djokovic match at the Olympics.

Stubbs got wind of RussoГўВҖВҷs harsh critique of her work and claimed his take was sexist. Even worse, Stubbs said on Twitter, ГўВҖВңDoes he comment on all the men that talk non stop on broadcasts. Probably not.ГўВҖВқ

As someone who listens to Russo every single day, I cannot tell you how bad and embarrassing a take this is from Stubbs.

Among the broadcasters Russo criticizes on a regular basis: Tony Romo, Kevin Harlan and Gus Johnson. Nobody is more of an equal-opportunity basher when it comes to broadcasters than Chris Russo.

This is called sexism at its best. Does he comment on all the men that talk non stop on broadcasts. Probably not.

3.When one bettor lost out on a four-leg home run parlay Sunday because Brent Rooker didnГўВҖВҷt go deep, he let the AГўВҖВҷs outfielder know about it.

The gambler probably didnГўВҖВҷt expect Rooker to respond, but he did and it was fantastic.

Brent Rooker had the time yesterday Г°ВҹВҳВӮ(via@Skunkii_Plays|@FDSportsbook)pic.twitter.com/Gm5jG1Ir4i

4.Did Diamondbacks broadcaster Steve Berthiaume misread this shirt or did he intentionally ad-lib to avoid any possible controversy? Either way, he probably wasnГўВҖВҷt thrilled the camera person stayed on the fan that long.

Did Steve Berthiaume accidentally think the kids shirt say "I Can't I'm A Moron" rather than "I Can't I'm Mormon" Г°ВҹВҳВӮГ°ВҹВҳВӮpic.twitter.com/fygelavJf8

5.Orioles catcher James McCann took a pitch to the face on Monday, which resulted in a bloody and ugly scene.

James McCann is a legend for this.pic.twitter.com/C6FBFVqF9a

Somehow, McCann STAYED IN THE GAME. While I understand the natural instinct is to praise McCann for his toughness, itГўВҖВҷs just a regular season MLB game, one of 162. Just go get your face fixed.

And this is what McCann looked like after the game.

James McCann is a legend.Г°ВҹВҺВҘ:@kylegoonpic.twitter.com/XMyvBhOsFk

6.The latest episode ofГӮSI Media With Jimmy TrainaГӮВ features a conversation with Fox NFL analyst, Greg Olsen.

Olsen shares his reaction to going viral thanks to a photo from Christian McCaffrey and Olivia CulpoГўВҖВҷs wedding, explains why he looked the way he did in that photo and shares his thoughts on wedding etiquette.

Olsen also talks about going into this NFL season as FoxГўВҖВҷs No. 2 analyst after being the No. 1 analyst the last two years, what his relationship is like with Tom Brady and winning the Sports Emmy for Best Sports Event Analyst two years in a row.

Other topics discussed with Olsen include whether Comedy Central missed a golden opportunity by not having him roast Tom Brady, what goes on at Tight End University, the best tight end of all time and much more.

Following Olsen, Sal Licata from WFAN and SNY joins me for our weekly ГўВҖВңTraina ThoughtsГўВҖВқ segment. This weekГўВҖВҷs topics include thoughts on CBS and FoxГўВҖВҷs NFL booths for 2024, Nick CastellanosГўВҖВҷ propensity for homering during big news days, Peacock raising its prices, ГўВҖВңVeepГўВҖВқ and much more.

You can listen to theSI Media With Jimmy TrainaГӮВ below or onГӮAppleГӮВ andГӮSpotify.

You can also watchSI Media With Jimmy TrainaonГӮSports IllustratedГўВҖВҳsГӮYouTube channel.

7.RANDOM VIDEO OF THE DAY: Happy 50th birthday to Hilary Swank, who is responsible for an episode ofThe Officethat would never be allowed to air today.

Be sure toГӮcatch up on past editions of Traina ThoughtsГӮВ and check out theГӮSports Illustrated Media PodcastГӮВ hosted by Jimmy Traina onГӮApple,ГӮSpotifyГӮВ orГӮGoogle. You can also follow Jimmy onГӮTwitterandInstagram.

Jimmy Traina is a staff writer and podcast host for Sports Illustrated. A 20-year veteran in the industry, heГўВҖВҷs been covering the sports media landscape for seven years and writes a daily column, Traina Thoughts. Traina has hosted the Sports Illustrated Media Podcast since 2018, a show known for interviews with some of the most important and powerful people in sports media. He also was the creator and writer of SIГўВҖВҷs Hot Clicks feature from 2007 to '13.

title: NBC Generating Huge Ratings for Early Portion of Paris Olympics
description: Viewership numbers are up in a massive way across the board for NBCГўВҖВҷs coverage of the Summer Games.
